title: Equilibrium distribution data for salicylic acid + water + solvent systems
journal: Journal of Applied Chemistry and Biotechnology
abstract: AbstractEquilibrium distribution data have been presented at 30 °C for the distribution of salicylic acid between water and each of the solvents benzene, toluene, xylene and kerosene. The equilibrium distribution plots reveal that except in the case of kerosene, the acid favours the solvent layer more than the aqueous phase at higher concentrations, i.e., above 0.04 wt‐ % acid content. At lower concentrations the acid prefers more the aqueous phase. In the case of kerosene, the acid favours only the aqueous phase uniformly at all concentrations. The lower hydrocarbons seem to be better suited for the extraction of salicylic acid from the aqueous phase.
